channel.title: Woni Spotts Research
channel.description: Anthropological research and book chapters from Naked Steps: A Timeless Panoramic Ancestral Odyssey.

item.title: Naked Steps Chapter: History of West Africa, Beauty and the Beasts
item.description: Along the Niger River and Sahel, early human migrations and prehistoric innovations rose into powerful kingdoms in West Africa. Through agriculture, commerce, and the trade of human cargo, empires in Ghana, Mali, and Songhai dominated the region.

item.title: Naked Steps Chapter: History of Japan and Korea
item.description: Ancient Northeast Asians and coastal Ancestral Australo-Melanesians shaped early populations across the Japanese archipelago. Proto-Koreanic and proto-Japonic cultures emerged through agriculture, metallurgy, and spiritual traditions.

item.title: Naked Steps Chapter: History of Australia
item.description: The chapter opens with a journey by Homo sapiens from East Africa through Eurasia and Southeast Asia. In the Sahul, a prehistoric supercontinent, Ancestral Australo-Melanesians carried the genetics of Neanderthals and Denisovans.
